If you'd like information on Zantedeschia aethiopica (Calla lily), here's a paragraph:

Zantedeschia aethiopica, or the Calla lily, is a flowering plant native to southern Africa. The Calla lily has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of the Calla lily include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calla lily include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat colds, flu, and respiratory issues.
